feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Hantavirus cruise passenger says she’s being forced to quarantine in Nebraska
entry.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said her isolation was needed to protect public health, though the CDC has recommended home quarantine under certain conditions.

entry.title: The Best Time to Take Vitamin D for Maximum Absorption, According to Health Experts
entry.summary: Should you take your vitamin D supplement in the morning or at night? Learn what health experts say about when it's best to get the most benefits.

entry.title: Cockroaches scurry around with thousands of pieces of bacterial genomes
entry.summary: Transferring genes across species doesn't just happen in microbes.

entry.title: Man hospitalized for weeks with tick-borne illness. What is Powassan virus?
entry.summary: What to know about Powassan virus after a man has been hospitalized with the serious tick-borne illness.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo could become worst in history, Africa CDC warns
entry.summary: The ⁠number of confirmed cases in ⁠the country has increased to 837, including 196 deaths.

entry.title: Northern California resident infected with extremely rare tick-borne illness
entry.summary: A Northern California resident was hospitalized with Rickettsia lanei, a newly recognized tick-borne bacterium linked to severe illness.

entry.title: Taking laxatives can help with memory and attention span problems: study
entry.summary: A new study out Monday may have found a way to flush the brain fog and attention issues that accompany depression down the toilet.

entry.title: Jamestown Canyon virus detected in Grand Rapids mosquito
entry.summary: The Kent County Health Department says at least one mosquito collected from western Grand Rapids has tested positive for Jamestown Canyon virus.

entry.title: AI and brain-computer interface allow speechless ALS patient to work a full-time job
entry.summary: The hardware isn't new, but a UC Davis research team's machine learning-powered method of translating brain activity in an ALS patient into sentences with 92% accuracy is

entry.title: Two simple habits may outperform a popular longevity wonder drug: study
entry.summary: A buzzy diabetes drug that’s been hailed as a longevity booster may have some competition.

entry.title: Weight loss drugs slash risk of 4 types of cancer by 50% or more, study finds
entry.summary: Scientists have found that GLP-1 medications, such as Ozempic, and Wegovy, is linked to a decrease in the overall risk of developing obesity-related cancers,...

entry.title: DRC’s Ebola outbreak may be worst ever, Africa CDC says
entry.summary: The head of Africa’s Centres for Disease ​Control and Prevention warned on ‌Tuesday that the Ebola outbreak in Congo could be the ​worst ever, saying that ​currently tens of thousands of ⁠contacts of those ill ​with the disease had not ​been traced.

entry.title: Live with a partner? You may be sharing more microbes than you think
entry.summary: A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.

entry.title: ‘Sad nipple syndrome’ is leaving women distressed — docs explain what’s going on
entry.summary: It’s when the nipple gets brushed, by someone or something, and a wave of intense negative emotion washes over. Descriptions range from “the deepest pit in my stomach” to upset, d…

entry.title: Happy Birthday, Sara Beth: Clayton Girl Continues Inspiring Others Through Cancer Journey
entry.summary: CLAYTON, N.C. — When yearbook signing day arrived at LifeSpring Academy, Sara Beth Bagley’s mother expected to see students exchanging signatures and saying goodbye for the summer. One of the school’s traditions is gathering all students – from kindergarten t…

entry.title: Researchers Warn Widely Prescribed Blood Pressure Drugs Could Be Harming Diabetic Kidneys
entry.summary: New research suggests that a common second-line blood pressure treatment may not be as kidney-friendly as previously assumed for some people with diabetic kidney disease.

entry.title: Popular Supplement Ingredient Linked to Shorter Lifespans in Men
entry.summary: Researchers found a puzzling male-only pattern that could reshape views on a widely used cognitive nutrient and aging over time.

entry.title: COVID-19 vaccine linked with lower risk of heart attack, stroke: Study
entry.summary: The COVID-19 vaccine lowered the risk of cardiovascular issues associated with the virus by around 40 percent, according to new research. The study, which was published in JAMA Internal Medicine on Monday, studied the incidence of medical events like strokes…

entry.title: Gen Z Isn't Enjoying Their 20s As Much As Gen X & Boomers Did For 9 Pretty Legit Reasons
entry.summary: Older people may think youth is a time to live your best life, but for Gen Z, their 20s are not particularly enjoyable at the moment.

entry.title: Column | This exercise ‘sweet spot’ is linked to greater longevity
entry.summary: A new study pinpoints how many minutes per week of strength training might help people avoid death from conditions including heart attacks and Alzheimer’s.

entry.title: Most Women Are Concerned About Heart Disease—but Miss This Early Sign
entry.summary: Cardiologists warn that many women aren’t aware that shortness of breath may be a signal of heart disease and share signs it’s time to seek medical attention.

entry.title: GLP-1 drugs linked to major drop in pancreatic, colorectal cancer risk
entry.summary: Scientists have found that GLP-1 medications, such as Ozempic, and Wegovy, is linked to a decrease in the overall risk of developing obesity-related cancers, especially with colorectal, pancreatic, and endometrial cancers.

entry.title: Watson IL woman undergoes groundbreaking procedure
entry.summary: Effingham's News and Sports Leader

entry.title: What Happens to YOur Body When You Take Collagen and Protein Powder Together
entry.summary: Find out what happens to your body when you take collagen and protein powder together. Sports dietitians weigh in on its safety, potential benefits and other things to consider.

entry.title: Covid vaccine linked to broad protections against heart conditions, study finds
entry.summary: The vaccine reduced cardiovascular conditions, hospitalizations and deaths of all causes, including those not linked to covid, researchers said.

entry.title: Magic Mushrooms And Alzheimer's: One Remarkable Case Raises New Questions
entry.summary: Magic mushrooms are better known for producing hallucinations and altering people's sense of reality than for treating brain diseases.

entry.title: Here's What You Should Know If You Use Your Hands To Clean Your Body In The Shower
entry.summary: Doctors reveal the washing method thats REALLY best for your skin — and it might surprise you.

entry.title: 5 Commonly Missed Signs Of Bowel Cancer
entry.summary: What if your symptoms are more than just stress or IBS?

entry.title: Common vitamin may influence brain aging in ways scientists didn't expect
entry.summary: New Japanese research links higher vitamin C blood levels to better-preserved brain structure and stronger neural connections in older adults.

entry.title: Parents are on devices during meals even more than their kids. Here’s how to rethink family dinner
entry.summary: Once the school pick-up routine begins in the afternoon, it can feel like mental arithmetic getting everyone to the right place at the right time: One child has math tutoring from 2 to 4 p.m., and then it’s straight to soccer practice from 4:30 to 7. The othe…

entry.title: Column | The dirty secret in your soap: Antibacterial soap may be breeding superbugs
entry.summary: Don't fall for hygiene theater: Antibacterial products aren't better than regular soap and water.

entry.title: Bristol scientist researching blood cancer after mum's diagnosis
entry.summary: A Bristol scientist is looking into a type of blood cancer after her mum fell ill with the disease.

entry.title: Ozempic was found in a 2025 clinical trial to reduce measurable biological age in adults by approximately 3 to 5 years — with improvements in inflammation, cardiovascular health, and kidney function — meaning a drug originally designed to manage blood s
entry.summary: The drug currently sweeping through the world’s prescription markets as a treatment for diabetes and obesity may turn out to do something its manufacturer never quite designed it for. In July 2025, a research team led by Michael J. Corley of UC San Diego, wit…

entry.title: Brown: Ticks are on the rise in Minnesota ― and so are the diseases they carry
entry.summary: These arachnids now occupy more of Minnesota than ever, and their pathogens are growing more diverse.

entry.title: Benadryl Challenge: Teen left brain dead after viral TikTok
entry.summary: A 15-year-old girl has been left with "no brain activity" in an ICU after attempting a viral TikTok challenge.

entry.title: Think higher SPF means better? Experts say not always
entry.summary: These are the common mistakes dermatologists want you to stop making when it comes to sunscreen, especially for people over 40.

entry.title: Copper drug clears toxic Alzheimer’s proteins and restores memory
entry.summary: A copper-based compound restored the brain’s ability to clear toxic Alzheimer’s proteins, dramatically reducing amyloid buildup and improving memory in laboratory experiments. The findings point to a potentially fast-tracked new treatment strategy because the…

entry.title: Women sleep better than men – but experience the opposite
entry.summary: Women are more likely than men to report poor sleep, even though objective measurements show that their sleep is better in several respects, according to new research from Karolinska Institutet.

entry.title: Monmouthshire mum says she may have avoided ovarian cancer if she lived in England
entry.summary: Heather Morgan's postcode meant she was ineligible for a test that could have revealed her risk.

entry.title: Scientist confronting the rising global threat of mosquitoes
entry.summary: Growing up in Tahiti, Anna-Bella Failloux saw first-hand the threat posed by mosquitoes: nearly a third of adults on the picturesque island once had swollen...

entry.title: RFK Jr. Orders American Exposed to Hantavirus to Stay Quarantined Against Medical Advice
entry.summary: Health secretary says cruise passenger Angela Perryman has to stay isolated in Nebraska despite CDC report advising otherwise

entry.title: The Laundry Room Staple Sparking Cancer Concerns Online — and What Experts Actually Say
entry.summary: Experts break down the research behind viral claims linking dryer sheets and fabric softeners to cancer

entry.title: ‘It’s really concerning’: Lakeland residents speak after 5 people, puppy attacked by rabid cat
entry.summary: Animal control and the Department of Health are working together on the multiple rabies exposures, and neighbors said this was quite the scare.

entry.title: Popular 'Ice' Vape Flavors May Have a Hidden Effect on Your Heart
entry.summary: For years, flavour additives in e-cigarettes have been largely viewed as a secondary concern compared with nicotine.

entry.title: Utah reports nearly 700 measles cases since outbreak began, threatens elimination status
entry.summary: In the year since measles began spreading across the United States, Utah has reported nearly 700 cases.The Utah Department of Health and Human Services has rep

entry.title: American doctor who recovered from Ebola arrives back in U.S., says he's "feeling well"
entry.summary: Dr. Peter Stafford, his wife, Rebekah Stafford, and their four children all arrived safely on Monday, according to Serge, a Pennsylvania-based Christian missions organization.

entry.title: Possible measles exposure in three Bay Area locations
entry.summary: Health officials warn citizens of a possible measles exposure at SFO, a San Jose Trader Joe's and International Halal Market on June 8.

entry.title: A 'Useless' Organ That Doctors Often Remove May Actually Fight Cancer
entry.summary: There's a small fatty gland that sits behind your sternum and is often said to be 'useless' in adulthood.

entry.title: Teen Hospitalized for Flesh-Eating Bacteria After Swimming with Siblings in Florida
entry.summary: Joziah Thompson “is currently fighting for his life” after contracting Vibrio vulnificus

entry.title: Slow breathing can influence brain activity and decision behavior
entry.summary: A new study from the German Institute of Human Nutrition Potsdam–Rehbruecke (DIfE) and Charité—Universitätsmedizin Berlin shows for the first time that targeted control of human breathing rhythm can influence decision behavior by modulating heart and brain fu…
